title: Support for Unicode in the MQL Connector
breadcrumbs: Support for Unicode in the MQL Connector
text: Support for Unicode in the MQL Connector The Uniface MQL connector can support Unicode. By default, Unicode support is disabled. MySQL versions 5.0 and 5.1 provide Unicode support through the utf8 and the ucs2 character sets, which support the Unicode Basic Multilingual Plane (BMP). MySQL 5.5 provides a full range of Unicode character sets— utf32 , utf16 , ucs2 , utf8mb4 , and utf8 . For more details about MySQL Unicode support, consult the MySQL documentation. The MQL connector provides Unicode support for the MySQL utf8 character set, which only supports the Unicode Basic Multilingual Plane (BMP). You can enable Unicode support by: Setting the connector option unicode=on —enables fields with wide-character packing codes (such as W10, SW* ) to be mapped to the utf8 character set. For more information, see Unicode . Setting $DEF_CHARSET=UTF8 —enables Unicode characters to be used in table names and column names, user names and passwords, the SQL Workbench and the sql ProcScript statement. It also sets the default character set for communicating with the MySQL Server to utf8 . Note: Set $DEF_CHARSET=UTF8 only if the default character set of the MySQL database is utf8 . Related Links Uniface Support for Unicode Uniface supports Unicode in the user interface, database storage and retrieval, sorting, case conversion, and data exchange. Uniface uses Unicode UTF-8 encoding for as its internal character set. unicode Enable or disable Unicode support in the MQL connector. $DEF_CHARSET Specify the default character set used for storing data in String fields with C packing code.
